alt: A candlelit dining room at evening service — bare wood tables, set linen, a soft tungsten glow on the far wall.
The bar at Maison Brûlée — backlit shelves of wine in warm amber, glassware catching the light.
A crudo starter dressed with herbs and citrus on a cream-rimmed plate — quiet, restrained, plated by hand.
A hand-rolled pasta main twirled on a rustic plate, dressed with grated cheese and torn herbs.
A rustic warm tart with poached fruit and cream, photographed in soft daylight on a marble bench.
A hand pouring red wine into a glass at a candlelit table — the slow moment that opens dinner.
A candid portrait of a chef in a white jacket in the kitchen, half-lit by overhead service light.
A line cook's hands plating at the pass under warm service lights — steam rising, a tweezer, a thumbprint of sauce.
The street facade of Maison Brûlée at twilight — warm yellow window glow spilling onto wet pavement.
The wood-fired oven at the heart of the kitchen — flames inside the brick arch, embers banked to one side.
Fresh market herbs and produce arranged on a wooden butcher block in soft afternoon daylight.
A two-top set for the evening — linen, a lit candle, two water glasses, a single bud in a small bottle.
The Maison Brûlée block at night — the restaurant window glowing warm against a quiet, unlit street.
